_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to re-read and edit our own texts and others' texts using agreed criteria for structure, language features and conventions.

_source.w: We are learning to re-read and edit our own texts and others' texts using agreed criteria for structure, language features and conventions.
y: Editing against criteria makes revision focused and productive rather than random.
Being able to edit both our own and others' work builds our critical literacy skills.
Consistent editing practice transforms us from good writers into excellent writers.
eg: We use an editing checklist that covers structure, language features, grammar and punctuation.
We re-read our text specifically looking for one thing at a time — first meaning, then structure, then conventions.
We give specific, constructive feedback to a partner's writing based on agreed criteria.
We reflect on the editing choices we make and explain why each change improves the text.
We compare edited and unedited versions of a text and discuss the improvements.
We develop our own criteria for editing based on the text type we are working with.
